title: pseudopanax scopoliae
intro: Pseudopanax scopoliae, also known as the parlor tree or wharawhara, is a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and act as a natural antiseptic. The plant's health benefits include its potential to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and its anti-inflammatory properties may also help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Pseudopanax scopoliae can be prepared as an herbal tea or infused oil, allowing for the extraction of its medicinal compounds for use in alternative remedies.
